prs.body:
## Pull Request Description This PR implements the **Animation: Parallax Tilt Effect** requested in issue #18. It introduces a 3D parallax tilt effect that simulates mouse-tracking using 100% pure CSS (no JavaScript). I implemented a 3x3 "Invisible Grid Hack," overlaying 9 transparent tracking zones (`.tilt-zone`) on top of the card container. By mapping the `:hover` states of these zones to the card below using the general sibling selector (`~`), the card smoothly rotates in 3D space (`rotateX`, `rotateY`) toward the user's cursor. Resolves #18 --- ## Type of Change - [x] ✨ New animation / hover effect - [ ] 🧩 New component - [ ] 📝 Documentation improvement - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix in an existing submission - [ ] Other (describe below) --- ## Submission Checklist > ⚠️ PRs that fail this checklist will be **closed without review**. - [x] All changes are inside `submissions/examples/parallax-tilt-effect/` - [x] Includes `demo.html` — self-contained, opens in browser with no server - [x] Includes `style.css` — raw CSS for the proposed feature - [x] Includes `README.md` — what it does, how to use it, why it fits EaseMotion CSS - [x] **No changes to `core/`** - [x] **No changes to `components/`** - [x] One feature per PR (no bundled unrelated changes) --- ## Feature Description **What does this add?** A highly interactive 3D parallax tilt effect that simulates mouse-tracking using pure CSS and an invisible 9-zone grid layout. **How does a developer use it?** ```html
Tilt Me

prs.body:
## Pull Request Description This PR implements the **Animation: Morphing Card Hover** utility requested in issue #14. It introduces a smooth, pure CSS animation that transitions a standard rectangular card into an organic, blob-like shape on hover using the 8-value `border-radius` property and `transition` effects. Resolves #14 --- ## Type of Change - [x] ✨ New animation / hover effect - [ ] 🧩 New component - [ ] 📝 Documentation improvement - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix in an existing submission - [ ] Other (describe below) --- ## Submission Checklist > ⚠️ PRs that fail this checklist will be **closed without review**. - [x] All changes are inside `submissions/examples/morphing-card-hover/` - [x] Includes `demo.html` — self-contained, opens in browser with no server - [x] Includes `style.css` — raw CSS for the proposed feature - [x] Includes `README.md` — what it does, how to use it, why it fits EaseMotion CSS - [x] **No changes to `core/`** - [x] **No changes to `components/`** - [x] One feature per PR (no bundled unrelated changes) --- ## Feature Description **What does this add?** A pure CSS morphing card hover effect that seamlessly transitions the container into an organic, blob-like shape. **How does a developer use it?** ```html

prs.title: Features/complete screens
prs.body:
features: Complete Student & Admin Flows
Implemented the full core functionality for CampusHub:

Auth: Login/Signup with role-based redirection (Student vs Admin).
Student: Event Feed, Event Details with Registration, My Events (Registered/Interested), and My Day timeline.
Admin: Dashboard to Create, Edit, and Delete events, plus view registered participants.
Tech: Built with React Native (Expo) and react-navigation.

prs.title: feat: automatically cleanup outdated autogenerated permission files
prs.body:
## Description

Implements automatic cleanup of outdated autogenerated permission files in the Tauri plugin build system.

Closes #14320

## Problem

The `tauri_plugin` build script generates permission files for commands but never removes outdated ones when commands are removed. This leads to:
- Stale permission files accumulating
- Runtime errors from permissions for non-existent commands  
- Particularly problematic for private plugins with gitignored autogenerated directories

## Solution

Added a [cleanup_outdated_permissions](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:245:0-299:1) function that:
1. Reads all permission files in the autogenerated commands directory
2. Compares against the current list of commands
3. Removes files for commands no longer in the active list

The cleanup is automatically called in [autogenerate_command_permissions](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:310:0-379:1) before generating new permissions, ensuring zero configuration is required.

## Changes

**File:** [crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs](cci:7://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:0:0-0:0) (+207 lines)
- Added [cleanup_outdated_permissions](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:245:0-299:1) function (~60 LOC)
- Modified [autogenerate_command_permissions](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:310:0-379:1) to call cleanup
- Added comprehensive test suite (4 test cases, ~150 LOC)

## Testing

✅ **Verified:**
- Code compiles successfully
- All existing tests pass (27/27)
- Core cleanup logic tested in Rust Playground
- Function properly removes outdated files while preserving current ones

**Test cases added:**
1. [test_cleanup_outdated_permissions](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:566:2-621:3) - Main functionality test
2. [test_cleanup_nonexistent_directory](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:623:2-631:3) - Edge case handling
3. [test_cleanup_empty_commands](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:633:2-661:3) - Empty command list handling
4. [test_autogenerate_calls_cleanup](cci:1://file:///Users/jeetsrivastava/Desktop/tauri/tauri-repo/crates/tauri-utils/src/acl/build.rs:663:2-705:3) - Integration test

## Checklist

- [x] Implementation follows project coding conventions
- [x] Comprehensive test coverage added
- [x] Documentation/comments included  
- [x] Backward compatible (no breaking changes)
- [x] Addresses issue requirements (100-200 LOC)

## Additional Notes

This feature benefits plugin developers who refactor their APIs and prevents runtime bugs from stale permissions. The implementation uses defensive programming with graceful error handling and has O(1) command lookup using `HashSet` for efficiency.

prs.body:

   - Configurable via HTTP_MAX_BODY_SIZE environment variable (default: 10 MB)
   - Returns HTTP 413 when request body exceeds limit
   - Comprehensive test coverage including unit and integration tests
   - Follows GoFr middleware patterns and coding standards

prs.title: Fix docs parser crash when prop.tags is undefined (#15764)
prs.body:
Fixed docs parser crash when prop.tags is undefined (#15764)

### Problem
The documentation parser in `docs/source/api.js` crashes with a `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'Symbol(Symbol.iterator)')` when processing JSDoc comments that don't have tags. This causes the entire documentation build process to fail.

### Root Cause
At line 273 in `docs/source/api.js`, the code attempts to iterate over `prop.tags` without checking if it exists:
When the `dox` parser encounters JSDoc comments without tags, `prop.tags` is `undefined`, causing the iterator to fail.

### Solution
Added a defensive check before the iteration to skip properties without tags:

This allows the parser to gracefully skip entries without tags while continuing to process properly documented code.

prs.title: feat: include test duration in timeout error messages

labels.name: semver-major
labels.description: implementation requires increase of "major" version number; "breaking changes"

prs.body:
Enhance timeout error messages to include the actual test duration when available. This provides developers with more context when debugging slow tests that exceed their timeout threshold.

Changes:
- Modified _timeoutError() to accept optional duration parameter
- Track start time on runnable instance to calculate duration
- Include duration in error message when timeout fires or test finishes late
- Add comprehensive tests for duration inclusion in timeout errors

The error message now includes: 'Test ran for Xms' when duration is available, making it easier to understand how much longer the test took than the timeout.

- fixes #5543

issues.title: Add configurable request body size limiting middleware
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**  
Currently, GoFr does not provide a built-in way to limit the size of incoming HTTP request bodies. This can lead to:

- Potential DoS attacks by sending very large payloads  
- Excessive memory and CPU usage under heavy or malicious traffic  
- Unexpected performance degradation if clients accidentally send large bodies  

I’d like a framework-level solution so that every service does not have to implement this logic manually.

---

**Describe the solution you'd like**  
I would like GoFr to provide a configurable request body size limiting middleware, for example `BodySizeLimit`, with the following behavior:

- Enforce a maximum allowed request body size (default: **10 MB**)  
- Allow configuration via an environment variable, e.g. `HTTP_MAX_BODY_SIZE`  
- For requests exceeding the configured limit:
  - Return **HTTP 413 Payload Too Large**
  - Do not invoke the next handler in the chain
- Use existing GoFr middleware patterns (same function signatures / registration style)
- Optionally skip enforcing limits for methods that typically don’t have bodies (e.g. `GET`, `HEAD`, `DELETE`)

This middleware can be registered globally in the app, for example:

```go
maxBodySize := config.GetInt64("HTTP_MAX_BODY_SIZE")
app.Use(middleware.BodySizeLimit(maxBodySize))
